title: hallucination-stress-test-suite-and-recovery-playbook-2027
preview:
Overview
========

This document describes the Hallucination Stress Test Suite (HSTS) and an associated Recovery Playbook for operationalizing detection, triage, and automated mitigation of hallucination regressions across Redwood’s inference fleet (hosted, dedicated, private). The HSTS is intended to be integrated with the eval-mesh and probe-orchestrator pipelines to provide deterministic and stochastic stress probes that surface hallucination risk under real-world conditions: prompt priming changes, quantized kernels, KV-cache boundary events, and latency-preserving batching.

Goals
-----
- Provide a repeatable, versioned set of probes that exercise hallucination-prone behaviors.
- Define measurable metrics and pass/fail thresholds for gating rollouts.
- Prescribe triage steps and automated repair actions (prompt rewrites, quant-fallback, enhanced priming) to reduce incident MTTR.
- Integrate with model-onboarding checks and shadow-rollforward evaluation flows.

Scope
-----
Includes: multi-turn knowledge requests, attribute-grounding tests, temporal-fact queries, numeric-precision checks, hallucination amplification under low-context or adversarial priming, and functioncall hallucination detection.
Excludes: safety policy violations and content moderation tests (handled by the safety-sandbox).

Test taxonomy
------------
1) Fact-Falsification Probes (deterministic): prompts that request verifiable facts with expected ground truth answers. Useful for regression delta measurements after quantization or model version swaps.
2) Attributive Drift Probes (context-shift): prompts where entity attributes are swapped or obfuscated in successive turns to surface memory/KV-cache boundary failures.
3) Numeric-Precision Suite: arithmetic and date-time queries that expose precision erosion after low-precision kernels.
4) Adversarial Priming Benchmarks: small perturbations to priming instructions that cause confident but incorrect assertions.
5) Functioncall-Resolve Tests: functioncall-triggering prompts where hallucinated arguments or missing fields indicate generation-time confusion.

Metrics and thresholds
---------------------
We report the following metrics for each probe-run (per model/version/environment):
- Hallucination Rate (HR): fraction of probes where output contradicted accepted ground truth. (Target for production: HR <= 0.5% on canonical slices)
- Confidence-Calibration Drift (CCD): fraction where model assigns >0.8 confidence score to incorrect assertions (Target <= 0.1%)
- Anchor-Consistency Score (ACS): a syntactic/semantic consistency score across repeated runs (Target >= 95%)
- Reproducible-Flip Rate (RFR): probes that flip answer under seed/temperature perturbations (Target <= 2%)

Suggested gating thresholds for canaries and full rollouts:
| Stage | HR threshold | CCD threshold | ACS threshold | RFR threshold |
| Canary | 1.0% | 0.5% | 92% | 4% |
| Production rollout | 0.5% | 0.2% | 95% | 2% |

Test harness integration (engineers)
-----------------------------------
1. Add probe bundle to eval-mesh: place JSON spec in /eval-bundles/hsts/{model-family}/{bundle-version}.json. See Probe Spec Reference below.
2. Schedule: Canary runs -> daily for 48 hours; full-run shadowing -> every 6 hours for first 7 days post-rollout.
3. Execution engines: probe-orchestrator (deterministic), stochastic-sampler (for RFR), and shadow-sequencer (for mixed-traffic mirroring).
4. Artifacting: store outputs and seeds in the evaluation-asset-registry with artifact tags: hsts, model:{ver}, env:{hosted|dedicated|private}.

Probe Spec Reference (JSON example)
---------------------------------
```json
{
  "probe_id": "hsts-fact-001",
  "type": "fact-falsification",
  "input_template": "Who is the CEO of {company} as of {year}?",
  "expected": "{ground_truth}",
  "annotations": ["authoritative_source:wiki", "variant:abbreviated"]
}
```

Runbook: triage and automated recovery
-------------------------------------
When an HSTS run exceeds gating thresholds, follow the steps below. The goal is to rapidly determine whether the regression is model-logic, quantization, prompt-shift, or runtime (cache/batching) related.

Immediate steps (0–15 minutes):
1. Alerting: probing pipeline raises an incident to #ml-regressions with summary: model, ver, env, HR, CCD, top failing probe IDs.
2. Snapshot artifacts: ensure the evaluation-asset-registry snapshot is pinned for reproduce.
3. Run quick differential: execute failing probes against last-known-good model-version and current model-version in an isolated eval sandbox (use eval-cli --artifact-pin).

Hypothesis triage (15–60 minutes):
1. Quantization check: run quant-shadow replay (quant-shadow-fidelity-scan) with full-precision fallback to see if HR improves >50%. If yes, mark quant-failure and escalate to quant-tuning.
2. KV-cache boundary test: replay multi-turn failing probes with cache warmup disabled. If failures correlate, mark cache-boundary bug and create runbook task for runtime team.
3. Prompt-sensitivity: run adversarial-priming variants on the same model; if small phrasing changes change outputs, prioritize prompt-engineering short-term fixes.
4. Seed/Temperature variance: run stochastic-sampler at T=0.0, 0.2, 0.8. High RFR implies instability; may require temperature constraints or guardrails.

Automated mitigations (fast-fix via rollout tooling)
1. Canary rollforward block: set rollout policy to block further traffic to new variant (runtime: smart-routing set to prefer model:previous-stable).
2. Prompt rewrite shim: use the PromptRewrite sidecar (prompt-rewrite-config: enforce-anchoring=true) to force more specific grounding for affected routes for up to 72 hours.
3. Quant-fallback: toggle model-quant-fallback=true in deployment config to route suspect slices to FP16/FP32 pool.
4. Functioncall hardening: enable functioncall-argument-validation hooks to reject outputs with malformed or hallucinated args.

Longer-term remediation (owner teams)
1. If quant: initiate quant-tuner job with increased sample density on failing slices and add quant-profile exceptions.
2. If model-logic: file an issue for model-onboarding to require additional probe coverage during promotion (include failing probe set).
3. If prompt-sensitivity: add targeted prompt-catalog entries and annotate as 'anchoring-required' and add to the priming-registry.
4. If runtime: schedule capacity/team-runbook updates to fix KV-cache boundary handling or batching heuristics.

Operationalizing HSTS for customer-facing services
--------------------------------------------------
- Map probes to per-route SQC (service-quality-contract) entries in the per-route dashboards so Product can see HR deltas per customer.
- Tag high-risk tenant slices in the probe meta so dedicated/private deployments can maintain stricter gating (e.g., block quantized variants).
- Include HSTS pass/fail in release checklist for dedicated and private image promotions.

Monitoring and dashboards
-------------------------
Key dashboards to maintain:
- hsts-summary (overall HR by model-family, last 30d trend)
- hsts-canary-detail (failed-probe list for active canaries)
- hsts-quant-correlation (HR vs quant-profile across slices)

SLO and alerting rules (suggested)
----------------------------------
- Alert: model.HSTS.HR > 1.0% over 60m (warn) and > 2.0% over 60m (critical).
- Alert: CCD > 0.5% over 30m (critical).

Ownership and cadence
---------------------
- Owner: Applied ML / Evaluation team (primary), Runtime team (secondary for cache/batching issues), Quant team (secondary for quantization-related failures).
- Regular cadence: HSTS maintenance meeting every 2 weeks to rotate probe authorship, update ground-truth manifests, and review any high-severity incidents.

Appendix A — canonical failing probe examples
--------------------------------------------
1) Multi-turn attribution: 
- Prompt stream: 
  - Q1: "Alice moved to Cedar City in 2018; which company did she join after moving?"
  - Q2: "Which city did Alice move to in 2018?"
- Expected: Q1 -> 'Acme Systems (joined 2018)'. Q2 -> 'Cedar City'.
- Failure mode: model hallucinates a different company or fabricates dates.

2) Temporal facts: 
- Prompt: "What was the US federal funds rate in March 2019?"
- Expected source: FRED or treasury dataset entry.

Appendix B — ticket template for triage issues
---------------------------------------------
- Title: HSTS Regression: {model-family} {version} HR:{value}%
- Attach: evaluation-asset-registry snapshot id, failing probe list, seeds, quant-profile used, runtime flags.
- Severity: Blocker/High/Medium/Low
- Assignee: (runtime/quant/model-onboarding)

Related pages and links
-----------------------
- See also: evaluation-asset-registry, shadow-sequencer runbook, quant-profile-synthesis-and-safety-playbook (link in eval-bundle index).

Change history
--------------
- 2026-11-04: Initial draft and internal review.
- 2026-12-15: Added numeric-precision suite and functioncall tests.
- 2027-02-03: Finalized gating thresholds and automated mitigation flow; marked published.

If you are implementing this for a specific model or customer route and need help selecting seed bundles or probe density, contact Applied ML (alias: #applied-ml) or add an entry to the HSTS maintenance board.

title: Operational SLA: Bridging Quantization Fidelity Across Eval & Onboarding
preview:
Summary

Purpose:
This document defines an operational SLA (service-level agreement) that bridges our internal evaluation harness, prompt/benchmark suites, model onboarding checks, quality guardrails, and regression-triage procedures for quantized inference artifacts. The objective is to create a single, measurable contract for acceptable "fidelity delta" when migrating models or deploying mixed-precision/quantized variants into production.

Audience and scope:
- Applied ML engineers, runtime owners, SREs, and product owners responsible for model promotion and inference economics.
- Applies to all Redwood-managed serving paths (hosted, dedicated, private) where quantization or mixed-precision artifacts are used.
- Covers: pre-promotion evals, shadow validation, continuous monitoring, alerting thresholds, and rollback actions.

Definitions:
- Fidelity delta: the measured change in anchored evaluation metrics (coherence, instruction-following, task accuracy) between reference float32 artifact and candidate quantized artifact on a 1000-sample anchored slice. Expressed in absolute metric points or percentage relative to float baseline.
- Anchor slice: a small, curated subset of prompts representative of a tenant/service that is used as the canonical fidelity anchor for promotions.
- Quant tier: categorization of quant profiles by expected degradation risk (Tier A: low-risk, Tier B: moderate, Tier C: experimental).

Operational SLA matrix (summary):
| Workload profile | Quant tier | Max allowed fidelity delta (task acc) | Max allowed latency delta (p50) | Monitoring window |
| --- | ---: | ---: | ---: | --- |
| Safety-critical (billing, auth) | Tier A | 0.5% absolute | +10 ms | 24h rolling |
| High-coherence chat (customer-facing) | Tier A/B | 1.0% absolute | +15 ms | 48h rolling |
| Bulk embeddings / analytics | Tier B/C | 2.0% relative | +30 ms | 7d rolling |
| Experimental/proof-of-concept | Tier C | 5.0% relative | +50 ms | 14d rolling |

Measurement procedure (how we compute fidelity delta):
1. Locate anchor slice(s) for the service (see "Onboarding checklist" below).
2. Run reference agent (float32 or verified baseline) on slice; collect metrics: task_accuracy, response_coherence, functioncall_accuracy, rouge_score as applicable. Save baseline snapshot.
3. Run candidate quant artifact using the same inference runner and request shaping (same temp/stop sequences).
4. Compute delta = candidate_metric - baseline_metric. Report both absolute and percent.
5. Use bootstrap resampling (n=1000) to estimate 95% CI for delta; if interval crosses SLA limit, candidate fails.

Integration points with eval harness and prompt suites:
- Eval harness must expose an "anchor snapshot" API: POST /evals/snapshots with payload {slice_id, model_artifact, quant_profile}. Each snapshot produces an immutable trial id used in promotion decisions.
- Prompt/benchmark sets should include standardized labels and a per-prompt expected metric type (accuracy/coherence/functioncall). Keep anchor slices small (500-2k prompts) for fast iteration, and a larger stress set (10k+) for final gating.

Monitoring & alerting signals (runtime):
- Telemetry events to emit on candidate artifact rollout: quant_fidelity_snapshot, quant_runtime_profile, anchor_violation_alert.
- Key metrics to track at 1m/5m/1h windows:
  - anchor_task_accuracy.delta (abs points)
  - anchor_response_coherence.delta (0-1 normalized)
  - p50_latency.delta_ms, p95_latency.delta_ms
  - token_cost.per_1000.delta
- Alert rules (examples):
  - P1: anchor_task_accuracy.delta > SLA_limit for 2 consecutive 10m windows AND 95% bootstrap CI upper bound > SLA_limit -> trigger incident and automatic rollback.
  - P2: p95_latency.delta_ms > 2x baseline for 30m -> page SRE and enable canary throttling.

Rollback and mitigations (runbook):
1. When an anchor_violation_alert fires, the oncall engineer follows the triage checklist: verify telemetry, confirm slice identity, re-run snapshot locally against known baseline.
2. If violation reproduces, initiate automated rollback to previous artifact (see deployment orchestration runbook).
3. Open a regression ticket with: service owner, model artifact, quant profile, anchor snapshot id, bootstrap CI plot, and initial hypothesis. Tag ticket as "quant-regression" and include priority per SLA severity.
4. If rollback is not acceptable (e.g., cost constraints), apply temporary fallback: route 50% traffic to baseline, apply prompt rewrite mitigations for affected slice, and schedule emergency quant-tuning (see "Rapid quant tuning").

Onboarding checklist (for promoting a quant artifact):
- Ownership: assign model_owner and quant_owner.
- Anchor slices: register at least two anchor slices (functional and safety) and record baseline snapshots.
- Local eval: run n=5 seeds, bootstrap CI; collect metrics and record artifact id.
- Shadow run: run 72h shadow against 1% production traffic or tenant-equivalent workload. Collect telemetry and compare deltas.
- Resource & latency test: validate p50/p95 on target infra; confirm < SLA latency delta.
- Compliance & privacy: ensure anchor probes contain no PII or are redacted/masked per the privacy budget.
- Rollout plan: staged 10% -> 30% -> 100% with health gates (metric checks at each stage).

Rapid quant tuning cheat-sheet (triage path for small deltas that fail gating):
1. Re-evaluate tokenization and prefix handling; small tokenization differences often explode the coherence metric.
2. Try per-layer mixed-precision (keep first N and last M layers higher precision).
3. Apply low-intensity re-calibration: 1k synthetic calibration steps on anchor slice with noise-robust loss.
4. If tuning fails, classify as "Tier B -> Tier C" and follow slower remediation (more probes, human-in-the-loop review).

Telemetry schema (example event emitted by evaluator or runtime):
{
  "event": "quant_fidelity_snapshot",
  "model": "redwood-7b-v2",
  "artifact_id": "artifact-2026-12-05-7b-int8-mix",
  "quant_profile": "int8-mixed",
  "anchor_id": "billing-invoice-anch-2026-11",
  "metric": "task_accuracy",
  "baseline_value": 0.912,
  "candidate_value": 0.905,
  "fidelity_delta": -0.007,
  "delta_abs_points": 0.7,
  "bootstrap_ci_lower": -0.012,
  "bootstrap_ci_upper": -0.002,
  "timestamp": "2026-12-05T14:23:00Z"
}

(Raw JSON above is the canonical telemetry payload used by alerting rules. Ensure all fields are emitted; missing fields will cause the automated gate to default to conservative failure.)

Decision matrix (promotion action):
- If |delta| <= 50% of SLA_limit and CI excludes SLA_limit -> promote to Tiered Canary (10% traffic).
- If 50% < |delta| <= 100% of SLA_limit OR CI marginally crosses SLA_limit -> require engineering mitigation and re-run evals before promotion.
- If |delta| > SLA_limit or CI clearly exceeds SLA_limit -> fail promotion and trigger regression triage.

Regression triage handoff (required artifacts):
- Anchor snapshot IDs (baseline + candidate).
- Eval harness logs: seed manifests, RNG seeds, prompt ordering.
- Runtime traces: p50/p95 latency histograms, token breakdowns, KV-cache hit rates.
- Quant profile specification (YAML). Example spec below.

Quant profile example (minimal YAML):
profile_name: int8-mixed-lowloss
base_dtype: float32
quant_backend: rdx-quant-v2
layer_overrides:
  - layers: [0..3]
    dtype: float32
  - layers: [4..-1]
    dtype: int8
calibration:
  method: synthetic-noise
  steps: 1000
  lr: 1e-5
safety_flags:
  clamp_embeddings: true
  preserve_functioncall_head: true

Ownership & RACI (high level):
- Model Owner (Applied ML): responsible for baseline snapshot, anchor slice selection, and final promotion sign-off.
- Quant Owner (Runtime / Inference): responsible for building quant artifact, quant profile, and run-time integration.
- SRE: monitors telemetry, enforces rollout gates, triggers rollback.
- QA / Eval Team: maintains anchor pool, runs stress benchmarks, and signs off final fidelity acceptance.

Operational notes and caveats:
- Anchor slices are intentionally narrow: they are NOT a full representation of production; use them to detect regressions quickly. Always pair anchor checks with larger production-like stress scans before 100% rollout.
- For multilingual services, anchor coverage must include at least top-3 locales by traffic.
- Cost-driven rollouts that bypass SLAs require explicit executive approval and a compensating monitoring plan.

Change log:
- 2025-09-18: Initial draft (Priya Desai).
- 2026-03-02: Added bootstrap CI requirement and example telemetry (review by Ethan Park).
- 2026-12-05: Updated SLA matrix and rapid tuning cheat-sheet; added YAML quant spec example (Maya Lopez review).

Related reading and links:
- See internal eval harness API docs for snapshot endpoints and storage conventions.
- Refer to "edge-quant-fitness-onboarding-guide-2026.json" for per-device quant constraints.
- See "regression-impact-index-and-prioritization-framework-2024.json" for triage prioritization guidance.

Contact and next steps:
- For questions or to request new anchor slices, open an internal ticket and tag @applied-ml and @runtime.
- Next review scheduled Q1 2027 to align on emerging quant backends and latency-cost tradeoffs.

- Key metrics to track at 1m/5m/1h windows:
  - anchor_task_accuracy.delta (abs points)
  - anchor_response_coherence.delta (0-1 normalized)
  - p50_latency.delta_ms, p95_latency.delta_ms
  - token_cost.per_1000.delta
- Alert rules (examples):
  - P1: anchor_task_accuracy.delta > SLA_limit for 2 consecutive 10m windows AND 95% bootstrap CI upper bound > SLA_limit -> trigger incident and automatic rollback.
  - P2: p95_latency.delta_ms > 2x baseline for 30m -> page SRE and enable canary throttling.

Rollback and mitigations (runbook):
1. When an anchor_violation_alert fires, the oncall engineer follows the triage checklist: verify telemetry, confirm slice identity, re-run snapshot locally against known baseline.
2. If violation reproduces, initiate automated rollback to previous artifact (see deployment orchestration runbook).
3. Open a regression ticket with: service owner, model artifact, quant profile, anchor snapshot id, bootstrap CI plot, and initial hypothesis. Tag ticket as "quant-regression" and include priority per SLA severity.
4. If rollback is not acceptable (e.g., cost constraints), apply temporary fallback: route 50% traffic to baseline, apply prompt rewrite mitigations for affected slice, and schedule emergency quant-tuning (see "Rapid quant tuning").

Onboarding checklist (for promoting a quant artifact):
- Ownership: assign model_owner and quant_owner.
- Anchor slices: register at least two anchor slices (functional and safety) and record baseline snapshots.
- Local eval: run n=5 seeds, bootstrap CI; collect metrics and record artifact id.
- Shadow run: run 72h shadow against 1% production traffic or tenant-equivalent workload. Collect telemetry and compare deltas.
- Resource & latency test: validate p50/p95 on target infra; confirm < SLA latency delta.
- Compliance & privacy: ensure anchor probes contain no PII or are redacted/masked per the privacy budget.
- Rollout plan: staged 10% -> 30% -> 100% with health gates (metric checks at each stage).

Rapid quant tuning cheat-sheet (triage path for small deltas that fail gating):
1. Re-evaluate tokenization and prefix handling; small tokenization differences often explode the coherence metric.
2. Try per-layer mixed-precision (keep first N and last M layers higher precision).
3. Apply low-intensity re-calibration: 1k synthetic calibration steps on anchor slice with noise-robust loss.
4. If tuning fails, classify as "Tier B -> Tier C" and follow slower remediation (more probes, human-in-the-loop review).

Telemetry schema (example event emitted by evaluator or runtime):
{
  "event": "quant_fidelity_snapshot",
  "model": "redwood-7b-v2",
  "artifact_id": "artifact-2026-12-05-7b-int8-mix",
  "quant_profile": "int8-mixed",
  "anchor_id": "billing-invoice-anch-2026-11",
  "metric": "task_accuracy",
  "baseline_value": 0.912,
  "candidate_value": 0.905,
  "fidelity_delta": -0.007,
  "delta_abs_points": 0.7,
  "bootstrap_ci_lower": -0.012,
  "bootstrap_ci_upper": -0.002,
  "timestamp": "2026-12-05T14:23:00Z"
}

(Raw JSON above is the canonical telemetry payload used by alerting rules. Ensure all fields are emitted; missing fields will cause the automated gate to default to conservative failure.)

Decision matrix (promotion action):
- If |delta| <= 50% of SLA_limit and CI excludes SLA_limit -> promote to Tiered Canary (10% traffic).
- If 50% < |delta| <= 100% of SLA_limit OR CI marginally crosses SLA_limit -> require engineering mitigation and re-run evals before promotion.
- If |delta| > SLA_limit or CI clearly exceeds SLA_limit -> fail promotion and trigger regression triage.

Regression triage handoff (required artifacts):
- Anchor snapshot IDs (baseline + candidate).
- Eval harness logs: seed manifests, RNG seeds, prompt ordering.
- Runtime traces: p50/p95 latency histograms, token breakdowns, KV-cache hit rates.
- Quant profile specification (YAML). Example spec below.

Quant profile example (minimal YAML):
profile_name: int8-mixed-lowloss
base_dtype: float32
quant_backend: rdx-quant-v2
layer_overrides:
  - layers: [0..3]
    dtype: float32
  - layers: [4..-1]
    dtype: int8
calibration:
  method: synthetic-noise
  steps: 1000
  lr: 1e-5
safety_flags:
  clamp_embeddings: true
  preserve_functioncall_head: true

Ownership & RACI (high level):
- Model Owner (Applied ML): responsible for baseline snapshot, anchor slice selection, and final promotion sign-off.
- Quant Owner (Runtime / Inference): responsible for building quant artifact, quant profile, and run-time integration.
- SRE: monitors telemetry, enforces rollout gates, triggers rollback.
- QA / Eval Team: maintains anchor pool, runs stress benchmarks, and signs off final fidelity acceptance.

Operational notes and caveats:
- Anchor slices are intentionally narrow: they are NOT a full representation of production; use them to detect regressions quickly. Always pair anchor checks with larger production-like stress scans before 100% rollout.
- For multilingual services, anchor coverage must include at least top-3 locales by traffic.
- Cost-driven rollouts that bypass SLAs require explicit executive approval and a compensating monitoring plan.

Change log:
- 2025-09-18: Initial draft (Priya Desai).
- 2026-03-02: Added bootstrap CI requirement and example telemetry (review by Ethan Park).
- 2026-12-05: Updated SLA matrix and rapid tuning cheat-sheet; added YAML quant spec example (Maya Lopez review).

Related reading and links:
- See internal eval harness API docs for snapshot endpoints and storage conventions.
- Refer to "edge-quant-fitness-onboarding-guide-2026.json" for per-device quant constraints.
- See "regression-impact-index-and-prioritization-framework-2024.json" for triage prioritization guidance.

Contact and next steps:
- For questions or to request new anchor slices, open an internal ticket and tag @applied-ml and @runtime.
- Next review scheduled Q1 2027 to align on emerging quant backends and latency-cost tradeoffs.

title: Runtime Shadow Rehearsal and Localized Probe Synthesis Playbook
preview:
Summary

This playbook defines the runtime "shadow rehearsal" pattern: an operationalized workflow that (1) synthesizes localized evaluation probes targeted at tenant cohorts, locales, and latency bands; (2) runs those probes in a low-risk shadow mesh against quantized and mixed-precision artifacts; and (3) performs cost-aware rehearsal passes that validate quality, latency, and coherence before a real rollout. The goal is to detect regressions and quant-induced degradations earlier while bounding evaluation cost and preserving tenant privacy.

Scope

- Applies to model promotion and quantized rollouts for text generation and multi-turn chat services (service names: eval-orch, shadow-mesh, rehearsal-runner).
- Covers probe generation, seed selection, rehearsal execution, gating metrics, and rollback triggers.
- Intended for on-prem, private VPC, and hosted Dedicated deployments.

Why this pattern

- Longtail and locale-specific regressions often escape coarse global evals. Localized probes increase sensitivity to per-tenant failure modes.
- Running rehearsals against quantized artifacts in a shadow mesh simulates production mixing without impacting production traffic.
- Cost-aware caps and staged rehearsal passes allow teams to validate risk under budget constraints.

High-level components

1) Probe Fabricator (probe-fabricator)
   - Responsible for localized probe synthesis: locale-aware lexical meshes, few-shot priming templates, and context anchors.
   - Inputs: seed traffic fingerprints, tenant taxonomy, priming registry, localization lexicons.
2) Shadow Mesh (shadow-mesh)
   - Lightweight runtime that replays probes against quantized variants and the nominal FP32 baseline.
   - Provides instrumentation (response_coherence, fidelity_delta, q_loss, latency_p95, token_emissions).
3) Rehearsal Runner (rehearsal-runner)
   - Orchestrates rehearsal passes, enforces cost caps, aggregates metrics, and writes rehearsal artifacts to eval-orch ledger.
4) Gating Engine
   - Applies SLOs and guardrail rules. Produces pass/fail verdicts and recommended rollout decisions.

Localized probe synthesis strategy

- Taxonomy partitioning: split the tenant base into cohorts along three orthogonal axes: locale (en-US, en-GB, fr-FR, de-DE, ja-JP), intent surface (support, search, compose), and latency band (fast p95 < 150ms, medium 150–500ms, slow > 500ms).
- For each cohort generate probes across these buckets:
  - 40% anchor probes (real user anonymized seeds sampled from last 90 days)
  - 40% synthetic perturbations (paraphrases, tokenization edgecases, punctuation/noise injection)
  - 20% adversarial variants (contrastive prompts designed to stress hallucination and instruction-following)
- Probe counts per rehearsal pass are configurable; default targeted sample: 1k probes per cohort for small services, 10k for high-volume services.

Privacy and provenance

- Anchor probes must be redacted and hashed before leaving telemetry storage (hash-salt per rehearsal job). Raw PII is never exported.
- Provenance metadata attached to each probe includes cohort_id, seed_hash, probe_type, and priming_template_id.

Rehearsal execution modes (staged passes)

1) Dry-run (low-cost smoke)
   - Run: 1–2% of full probe set, quant variants only, no baseline comparison.
   - Purpose: surface obvious failures and infra incompatibilities. Cost cap: $X/day (set by team).
2) Contrast-pass
   - Run: 20% sample, run against FP32 baseline and quant variant(s). Compute fidelity_delta metrics and delta-significance tests.
3) Full-rehearsal
   - Run: 100% probe set, cohort-balanced. Includes heavier metrics (token-level divergence, functioncall stability).

Gating metrics and thresholds

We compute both statistical and decisionable thresholds. Primary signals:

- fidelity_delta (KL-style scoring + templated semantic similarity): fail if cohort mean fidelity_delta > 0.12 and throughput-corrected CI excludes zero.
- response_coherence (annotator-simulated score 0–1): fail if cohort P90 < 0.70 and drop vs baseline > 0.08.
- q_loss (quantization artifact score): fail if absolute q_loss increase > 0.05 and correlated with token_emission anomalies.
- latency_p95: fail if latency_p95 increase > 2x and exceeds target SLO band for that cohort.
- embedding_stability (for retrieval/rerank): fail if cosine shift > 0.06 on average for critical slices.

Decision matrix (simplified)

- If any cohort fails fidelity_delta AND response_coherence -> abort rollout for that cohort and open regression ticket.
- If only latency_p95 regression observed but coherence preserved -> engage ops throttling + capacity reroute and run narrow replay.
- If q_loss is high but coherence preserved for anchor-probes -> schedule quant-tuning pass and run targeted micro-rehearsal.

Runbook: step-by-step rehearsal for a scheduled quant rollout

1) Preflight (owner: ModelOnboard Team)
   - Create rehearsal job in eval-orch: include cohort list, probe budget, quant-variant ids, and expected cost cap.
   - Run quick smoke locally with rehearsal-runner dry-run mode: run command below.
2) Dry-run review (owner: Release Lead)
   - Confirm no infra errors in shadow-mesh logs, check instrumentation counters (request_e2e_errors < 0.5%).
3) Contrast-pass (owner: Evaluation Engineers)
   - Run contrast-pass, collect fidelity_delta, response_coherence, and q_loss.
4) Analysis (owner: Applied ML)
   - Run automated delta-significance test (see CLI step). Produce report, tag failing cohorts.
5) Decision (owner: Release Lead + Product)
   - If no failing cohorts -> proceed to phased production rollout.
   - If some cohorts fail -> open triage (link created) and either rollback quant artifact or schedule micro-tunings.
6) Post-rehearsal monitoring (owner: SRE)
   - Enable rehearsal-derived sentinels for 72 hours post-rollout and maintain elevated sampling of failed cohorts.

CLI examples

# Start a dry-run rehearsal (example)
rehearsal-runner start --job-id rjr-20260712-01 --cohorts en-US:support:fast,de-DE:compose:medium --mode dry-run --probe-budget 200 --quant-variant qv-2.1 --cost-cap 200

# Run contrast-pass and upload report
rehearsal-runner start --job-id rjr-20260712-02 --mode contrast --probe-budget 2000 --quant-variant qv-2.1 --baseline mv-1.0 --report-bucket s3://eval-orch/reports/rjr-20260712-02

YAML rehearsal job manifest (example)

job_id: rjr-20260712-02
owner: model-onboard
cohorts:
  - id: en-US-support-fast
    locale: en-US
    intent: support
    latency_band: fast
    probe_target: 1000
  - id: de-DE-compose-medium
    locale: de-DE
    intent: compose
    latency_band: medium
    probe_target: 1000
quant_variants:
  - qv-2.1
baseline: mv-1.0
mode: contrast
cost_cap_usd: "1500"
retention_days: "30"

Interpretation and reporting

- Reports include cohort-level summaries, ranked failure slices, example failing probes, and suggested remediation actions. Attachments include raw witness tokens (hashed) and aggregated diff visualizations.
- Use the provided report template (report-template-v2) for consistency.

Cost control and budgeting

- Rehearsal-runner enforces a hard cost cap at job-level. If cost consumption reaches 90% of cap during a mid-pass, execution pauses, and a partial report is emitted for review.
- Default cost guidance: small service full-rehearsal ~ $800–1,200; large service ~ $4k–8k depending on probe complexity and annotator simulation.

Integration with quant profiles and eval-harness

- Rehearsal jobs reference quant profile IDs from the quantization catalog. Rehearsals must include quant metadata (bitwidth, kernel-profile, stash-policy).
- The probe-fabricator consults the eval-harness priming registry to select few-shot templates, ensuring parity between rehearsal prompts and production priming.

Failure triage and escalation playbook

1) Triage run (owner: Applied ML)
   - Label failing probes by failure mode (hallucination, truncation, functioncall-mismatch, tokenization-error).
2) Cross-team incident (owner: Release Lead)
   - If multiple cohorts fail fidelity_delta or coherence -> declare a Rehearsal Incident (severity: S2) and run causal regression investigation.
3) Rollback policy
   - Immediate rollback recommended when anchor-probe P90 coherence drops below 0.6 for >3 cohorts or when similarity drift >= 0.18 with statistical significance.

Postmortem artifacts and follow-ups

- Create a rehearsal incident dossier containing: rehearsal job manifest, cohort failure matrix, top 50 failing probes, annotator sample, and timeline of actions. Attach to the central incident tracker and link to model-promote PR.
- Follow-ups commonly include: micro-quant-tune, priming-template adjustment, or probe-corpus augmentation.

Ownership and contacts

- Probe Fabricator: Applied ML (owner: Priya Natarajan)
- Rehearsal orchestration: Platform Eval (owner: Marcus Holt)
- Gating & artifacts: ModelOnboard (owner: Sofia Alvarez)
- SRE & post-deploy monitoring: Platform SRE (owner: Liam Chen)

Appendices

A) Metrics table (columns: metric, description, alert-threshold, owner)

metric | description | alert-threshold | owner
fidelity_delta | semantic divergence vs baseline | >0.12 cohort mean | Applied ML
response_coherence | simulated annotator coherence 0–1 | cohort P90 < 0.70 | Evaluation
q_loss | quant artifact amplitude | absolute increase > 0.05 | ModelOnboard
latency_p95 | 95th percentile E2E latency | >2x baseline for cohort | SRE
embedding_stability | avg cosine shift per slice | >0.06 | Retrieval Team

B) Delta-significance command (example)
# runs bootstrap CI test comparing metric streams
python tools/delta_test.py --baseline reports/mv-1.0/metrics.json --variant reports/qv-2.1/metrics.json --metric fidelity_delta --alpha 0.05 --output reports/delta-ci.json

C) Quick checklist before scheduling a full rehearsal

- [ ] Manifest created and reviewed, probes budgeted and redaction confirmed.
- [ ] Quant variant artifacts uploaded to artifact store and kernel-profile declared.
- [ ] Shadow mesh capacity validated and can accept N concurrent rehearsal workers.
- [ ] Cost cap set and billing owner notified.
- [ ] Reviewers assigned and report template linked.

Related links and references

- See: /confluence/applied-ml-and-evals/quantization-profiles/quant-shadow-cohort-strategy-and-rollback-playbook-2026 for quant fallback patterns.
- See: /confluence/applied-ml-and-evals/eval-harness/shadow-to-prod-eval-continuity-guidelines-2026 for end-to-end shadow orchestration.

Document revision history

- 2026-02-10: initial draft (S. Alvarez)
- 2026-04-02: added cohort taxonomy and cost guidance (Marcus Holt)
- 2026-07-12: finalized gating thresholds and rollout runbook (S. Alvarez, Priya N.)

title: Eval Sprint Orchestration and Annotator Rota
preview:
Summary:
This runbook defines the lightweight sprint-oriented orchestration model we use to deliver targeted eval work (bug-hunting, quant checks, prompt stress runs, and slice-specific labeling) with a short turnaround. It combines a deterministic scheduler in the eval harness, an annotator rota (part-time reviewers/labelers), and a distributed telemetry gate so results can be promoted into the model-onboarding pipeline or trigger regression triage.

Goals:
- Deliver small, high-impact eval scopes in 2–5 business day sprints.
- Minimize lead time between discovery (telemetry alert) and annotated evidence for action.
- Keep annotator overhead low with clear instructions and a rotating, documented rota.
- Ensure traceability from samples -> annotations -> evaluation artifacts in the eval harness.

Scope:
This document covers: sprint cadence and roles, prioritization criteria, annotator rota templates, interaction with the eval harness (config snippets), acceptance gates and guardrails, telemetry to collect, and escalation steps for regressions. It does NOT replace full promotion runbooks for major model releases.

Definitions:
- Sprint: 2–5 business days focused evaluation burst.
- Sprint owner: engineering lead who coordinates harness config, evaluation metrics and artifact ingestion.
- Annotator rota: scheduled, part-time annotators (internal contractors or cross-functional engineers) assigned to the sprint.
- Evidence bundle: the collection of raw samples, annotations, evaluator outputs, and delta metrics stored in the evaluator artifact registry.

Roles and responsibilities:
1) Sprint owner (1x) — configures harness job, writes annotation spec, coordinates reviewers, owns outcome decision.
2) Lead annotator (1x) — first-pass QA of annotations, communicates ambiguities back to owner.
3) Annotators (2–6) — perform labeling according to spec. Recommend mixing product-facing and infrastructure-facing reviewers for balanced perspective.
4) Data engineer (optional) — prepares sample slices and ensures privacy redaction.
5) Oncall (applied-ml) — available for fast regression triage if evidentiary signals point to production impact.

Sprint cadence (recommended):
1. Day 0: Triage & kickoff (Sprint owner) — define slice, success metrics, sample size (usually 200–2000 depending on severity), annotation budget.
2. Day 1: Harness config + annotator briefing — owner posts harness job and annotation spec; schedule rota.
3. Day 2–3: Annotation window — annotators label; lead annotator runs spot checks.
4. Day 4: Aggregation + analysis — sprint owner runs evaluator job, compiles evidence bundle, and runs the promotion checklist.
5. Day 5: Decision & handoff — publish results, file follow-up (deck, ticket, or regression runbook).

Prioritization rubric (how sprints are chosen):
- P0 (urgent): production errors or security-sensitive failures. Target sprint < 24h. Sample size 1k–2k.
- P1 (high): measurable quality drop tied to a route/model; sprint 2–3 days.
- P2 (medium): exploratory probes for onboarding; sprint 3–5 days.
- P3 (low): cosmetic or long-term calibration work; schedule into regular backlog.

Annotation spec template (must be included in every sprint):
- Title: short descriptive name.
- Objective: one-sentence reason for the sprint.
- Population: tenant(s), routes, time window, filters.
- Labels: enumerated set with definitions and canonical examples.
- Ambiguity rules: tie-breaker instructions and escalation flow.
- Metadata requirements: tenant_id (redacted hashed id), model_version, prompt_context, token_count, seed_id.

Sample annotator rota (ASCII schedule):
| Day | Role | Person | Window (PST) | Notes |
|-----|------|--------|--------------|-------|
| 1 | Sprint owner | Priya Shah | 09:00–10:00 | Kickoff, spec publish |
| 1–3 | Annotator A | Marco Rivera | 11:00–15:00 | 3 hr shifts daily |
| 1–3 | Annotator B | Ava Chen | 12:00–16:00 | overlapping coverage |
| 2–3 | Lead annotator | Daniel Kim | 15:00–17:00 | spot checks, QA |
| 4 | Data engineer | SRE Oncall | on demand | aggregation & redaction |

Annotation quality guardrails:
- Inter-annotator agreement (IAA): target Cohen's kappa >= 0.6 for binary labels, or Krippendorff alpha >= 0.5 for multi-class.
- Minimum per-label counts: 30 examples per label for basic statistical validity.
- Annotation latency SLA: 80% of assigned tasks completed within 24 hours during sprint.
- Lead annotator must flag ambiguous instructions within 12 hours; ambiguous samples halted until clarified.

Eval harness integration (example):
- Job manifest (YAML):
```yaml
job_id: sprint-2026-42
cohort: user-facing-rerank
samples_source: s3://evaluator/slices/user-facing-rerank/2026-05
annotation_spec: /confluence/applied-ml-and-evals/specs/sprint-2026-42.yaml
annotation_target: evaluator://artifact-registry/sprints/sprint-2026-42/annotations
timeout_ms: 600000
parallel_workers: 8
```
- CLI commands:
  - Create job: rwinf-eval-cli create --manifest sprint-manifest.yaml
  - Monitor job: rwinf-eval-cli status job-id
  - Export evidence bundle: rwinf-eval-cli export --job job-id --format tar.gz --out /tmp/sprint-2026-42-evidence.tar.gz

Acceptance gates and promotion checklist (binary pass/fail):
1. Artifact completeness: annotation_target contains >= configured sample_count and at least 95% of items have required metadata.
2. Quality thresholds: IAA and per-label counts meet guardrails.
3. Metric delta: defined primary metric delta (e.g., top-1 accuracy, hallucination rate) is computed and documented.
4. Privacy check: all PII redaction flags cleared by Data engineer.
5. Traceability: each annotated item links to original sample and harness job id.

Telemetry signals to capture (minimum):
- per-sample: latency_ms, prompt_length_tokens, model_version, route, tenant_bucket.
- annotator-level: labels_assigned, time_spent_seconds, flagged_ambiguous_count.
- job-level: job_duration_seconds, success_rate, retry_count, artifact_size_bytes.

Escalation & regression triage flow:
1. If sprint uncovers a regression with P0/P1 impact, open a regression-runbook ticket and ping applied-ml oncall. Include evidence bundle link.
2. For unclear degradations, run targeted shadow runs (see related eval-harness shadow-run procedures) and expand sample size to 5–10x.
3. If quantization or runtime change suspected, attach quant-profile snapshot and kernel/profiler traces to the ticket.

Operational best practices and tips:
- Prefer multiple short sprints over a single multi-week pass for fast feedback loops.
- Always include at least one cross-functional annotator (product or customer success) to reduce annotation drift.
- Use the harness parallel_workers to shape annotator queue throughput; keep worker_count roughly equal to active annotators to avoid long queuing.
- Archive evidence bundles with semantic names and the sprint manifest for reproducibility.

Template: post-sprint publish checklist (owner):
1. Link evidence bundle in the sprint Confluence page.
2. Attach evaluation notebook with metric deltas and visualizations.
3. Create follow-up ticket (if required) with remediation owner and ETA.
4. Update model-onboarding intake if the sprint is part of an onboarding path.
5. Rotate annotator rota and record lessons learned (2–3 bullets).

Appendix: sample annotation instruction excerpt
- Label categories (binary):
  - OK: response properly answers the prompt, no major hallucination, tone appropriate.
  - FAIL: incorrect facts, fabrication, or unsafe content.
  - AMBIG: response is ambiguous; annotator must use the AMBIG label and add a 1–2 sentence rationale.

- Short rationale examples: "Response claims nonexistent legislation (FAIL)"; "Model omitted required numerical field but intent clear (AMBIG)".

If you have questions or want to run a pilot sprint against a specific route, add a comment below or contact the Sprint owner list: applied-ml-sprints@redwood.ai.

Summary:
This runbook defines the lightweight sprint-oriented orchestration model we use to deliver targeted eval work (bug-hunting, quant checks, prompt stress runs, and slice-specific labeling) with a short turnaround. It combines a deterministic scheduler in the eval harness, an annotator rota (part-time reviewers/labelers), and a distributed telemetry gate so results can be promoted into the model-onboarding pipeline or trigger regression triage.

Goals:
- Deliver small, high-impact eval scopes in 2–5 business day sprints.
- Minimize lead time between discovery (telemetry alert) and annotated evidence for action.
- Keep annotator overhead low with clear instructions and a rotating, documented rota.
- Ensure traceability from samples -> annotations -> evaluation artifacts in the eval harness.

Scope:
This document covers: sprint cadence and roles, prioritization criteria, annotator rota templates, interaction with the eval harness (config snippets), acceptance gates and guardrails, telemetry to collect, and escalation steps for regressions. It does NOT replace full promotion runbooks for major model releases.

- Sprint: 2–5 business days focused evaluation burst.

- Evidence bundle: the collection of raw samples, annotations, evaluator outputs, and delta metrics stored in the evaluator artifact registry.

title: Few-shot priming economics and sample compression guide
preview:
Summary:
This guide documents Redwood's recommended approach to designing few-shot prompts and compressed sample sets for production evals. It balances quality (coherence, instruction-following) against unit-costs (tokens per probe, annotation overhead) and operational constraints (throughput, latency for streaming evals). The guidance is intended for evaluator engineers, model-onboarding owners, and probe stewards.

Goals:
- Reduce per-probe token cost by 20–60% without regressing key quality slices (intent accuracy, safety sentinel pass-rate).
- Provide an auditable compression workflow so that downstream continuous evals and shadow deployments use reproducible priming sets.
- Define metrics and an acceptance gate for compressed few-shot sets.

Background and tradeoffs:
Few-shot priming increases model context and improves alignment accuracy on many instruction tasks, but it also inflates token usage and can amplify quantization sensitivity. Compression here means generating a smaller representative set of examples or a condensed priming representation (templates, canonicalized examples, or sketch encodings) that preserves signal for the target slices.

Key principles:
1) Slice-first fidelity: prioritize preserving performance on high-priority slices (billing-sensitive, safety-critical) when compressing.
2) Incremental compression: perform staged compression with an explicit acceptance gate at each step.
3) Telemetry-driven selection: use real traffic fingerprints and evaluator telemetry to pick candidate exemplars.
4) Quant-compatibility testing: run compacted priming through quantized-shadow evaluation to detect sensitivity early.

Definitions:
- exemplar: a single few-shot example used in the prompt.
- compaction: any operation that reduces token footprint of a priming set (e.g., truncation, templating, summarization).
- fidelity delta: observed change in slice-level metric after compaction (positive or negative).

Recommended workflow (high level):
1. Assemble seed exemplar pool from: production probes, human-curated anchor examples, and synthetic perturbations. Target pool size: 500–5k per slice.
2. Compute exemplar fingerprints: token length distribution, semantic embedding, label anchoring, and contextual importance score.
3. Rank exemplars per slice using a hybrid score: w1 * embedding-diversity + w2 * label-coverage + w3 * recency. Default weights: [0.5, 0.3, 0.2].
4. Apply staged compaction strategies (see table).
5. Run micro-canary evals across: float baseline, INT8/INT4 quant-shadow, cached vs cold contexts.
6. Accept/reject with acceptance gate (see Acceptance gate).

Compaction strategies (choose 1–3, apply incrementally):
| Strategy | Typical reduction | When to use | Risks |
|---|---:|---|---|
| Truncate long examples (keep minimal context) | 20–40% | Long narrative examples with low label density | may omit crucial reasoning steps |
| Template extraction (replace verbose spans with slot templates) | 30–70% | Repetitive instruction scaffolds across examples | over-simplification, instruction ambiguity |
| Example summarization (LLM-based abstractive) | 40–80% | Long multi-paragraph demos | hallucination in summaries |
| Representative exemplar selection (diversity-based sampling) | 50–90% | Large exemplar pool with redundancy | misses rare edge behaviors |
| Sketch encoding (compact structured tokens for reasoning chains) | 60–95% | Controlled tasks with repeatable reasoning patterns | requires runtime deterministic decoder |

Config example (YAML) - compress-config.yaml:
---
slice: "account-inquiry"
seed_pool_path: "s3://eval-probes/seed/account-inquiry-v2.jsonl"
fingerprint: [embedding_model: "embed-v3", tokenizer: "cl100k_base"]
rank_weights: [0.5, 0.3, 0.2]
max_tokens_after_compaction: 2400
stages:
  - name: "template-extract"
    strategy: "template-extraction"
    params: {max_template_slots: 3}
  - name: "select-representatives"
    strategy: "k-center-sampling"
    params: {k: 64, diversity_metric: "cosine"}
  - name: "summarize-examples"
    strategy: "abstractive-summarization"
    params: {max_length: 80, model: "summarizer-2b"}
acceptance_gate:
  target_metric: "intent_accuracy"
  delta_threshold: -0.015  # reject if accuracy drops >1.5%
  additional_checks: ["safety_sentinel_pass", "embedding drift < 0.02"]
---

Acceptance gate (detailed):
- Primary metric: slice-level intent accuracy or task-specific correctness.
- Allowed fidelity delta: default -1.5% relative to full-shot baseline for non-S safety slices. For S (safety/finance/legal) slices the allowed delta is -0.2%.
- Checkpoints:
  1. Local micro-canary: 256 probes, compare float baseline (full shot) vs compressed.
  2. Shadow canary: run 2,000 probes against quant-shadow configuration.
  3. Production probe escrow (optional): limited roll-forward on 0.1% of traffic for 24 hours.
- Rollback triggers: fidelity delta > threshold, safety sentinel failures increase by >0.1%, or latency regression > 25ms p95.

Metrics and telemetry to capture:
- token_savings_pct (per-probe)
- fidelity_delta_by_slice (intent_accuracy, topk_coverage, safety_pass_rate)
- quant_sensitivity_score (change from float to quantized on critical prompts)
- exemplar_coverage (percent of original labels represented)
- sampling_entropy (indicator of selection diversity)

Procedural checklist: compress-and-certify (numbered):
1. Create seed pool and store versioned manifest in S3 with sha256 checksums.
2. Run fingerprinting job (embedding + tokenizer stats) and persist results to probe-catalog.
3. Run automated ranking; inspect top-100 exemplars manually for critical slices.
4. Execute first compaction stage (template extraction).
5. Launch micro-canary: 256 examples, run against float baseline. Record metrics and attach run artifacts.
6. If pass, execute second stage (representative selection) and run shadow canary.
7. If shadow canary passes, attach compressed set to promotion PR and request two reviewers (probe steward + model owner).
8. On approval, schedule a 24h prod-escrow roll-forward (0.1% traffic) with observability hooks.
9. If production signals are green, tag the compressed set as promoted and update eval catalog.
10. Archive the original seed pool and link to compressed set for traceability.

Pseudocode: exemplar ranking (simplified)
```
def score(example):
    emb_div = diversity_score(example.embedding, shard_embeddings)
    label_cov = label_coverage(example.labels)
    recency = recency_score(example.timestamp)
    return 0.5*emb_div + 0.3*label_cov + 0.2*recency

ranked = sorted(pool, key=score, reverse=True)
selected = k_center_sample(ranked, k=K, metric="cosine")
```

Acceptance test matrix (decision table):
| Stage | Dataset size | Baseline | Quant modes | Accept if | Action |
|---|---:|---|---|---|---|
| micro-canary | 256 | float | none | delta >= -0.015 for non-S slices | proceed |
| shadow-canary | 2,000 | float | INT8, INT4 | all quant modes within thresholds | proceed to PR |
| prod-escrow | 0.1% traffic | quantized serving | INT8 | no safety sentinel regressions, p95 latency within 25ms | promote |

Ownership and roles:
- Probe Steward: owns seed pool curation and manual inspection (team: applied-ml).
- Evaluator Engineer: owns ranking pipeline and micro-canary execution (team: eng-evals).
- Model Owner: final approval on promotion PR and rollout gating.
- SRE/Observability: ensures telemetry hooks and alerting during prod-escrow.

Rollout checklist for model onboards that will use compressed few-shot sets:
- Add compressed primer reference in service eval-contracts.yaml.
- Include a quant-sensitivity job in CI that runs on PRs (INT8 + INT4).
- Instrument per-route token_savings_pct and fidelity_delta_by_slice to Redwood Console dashboards.

Troubleshooting and recovery patterns:
- If quant_sensitivity_score spikes after compaction: revert to previous primer and run targeted exemplar augmentation for the adversarial sub-slice.
- If safety sentinel failures increase: immediately pause roll-forward, revert serving primer, and open an incident with applied-ml and safety SMEs.
- If latency p95 increases during prod-escrow: evaluate whether decoder complexity from sketch encodings is the cause; prefer template-extraction fallback.

Auditability and reproducibility:
- All compression runs must produce a manifest: {seed_manifest_sha, compaction_steps, random_seed, compressed_set_sha}.
- Store manifests in eval-catalog S3 and link the compressed_set_sha from the model promotion PR.

Open questions and future work:
- Better automated exemplar-to-template extraction that preserves reasoning chains without hallucination.
- Compact sketch encodings that are runtime-decodable across different serving runtimes.
- Integration of exemplar-level explainability signals to prefer exemplars with clear token-level attribution for decisions.

References and related docs:
- See eval-harness throughput/fidelity playbook for aligning micro-canary sizes.
- See quantization-profiles checklist for mixed-precision rollout guidance.

Appendix: quick CLI snippets
- Fingerprint job (example):
  rci eval-fingerprint --input s3://eval-probes/seed/account-inquiry-v2.jsonl --embed-model embed-v3 --out s3://eval-catalog/fingerprints/account-inquiry-v2.jsonl
- Run micro-canary:
  rci canary-run --set compressed-v1 --size 256 --mode float --out s3://eval-artifacts/canary/account-inquiry/micro-2026-03-10.json

Contact and ownership:
- Document owner: Priya Natarajan (applied-ml)
- Reviewers: Marco Alvarez, Diane Chen
- For operational help: ping #eng-evals on Slack and tag the oncall evaluator engineer.

Change log:
- 2026-02-12: created initial draft.
- 2026-03-10: updated acceptance thresholds and added quant-sensitivity_score metric.

|---|---:|---|---|---|---|
| micro-canary | 256 | float | none | delta >= -0.015 for non-S slices | proceed |
| shadow-canary | 2,000 | float | INT8, INT4 | all quant modes within thresholds | proceed to PR |
| prod-escrow | 0.1% traffic | quantized serving | INT8 | no safety sentinel regressions, p95 latency within 25ms | promote |

Ownership and roles:
- Probe Steward: owns seed pool curation and manual inspection (team: applied-ml).
- Evaluator Engineer: owns ranking pipeline and micro-canary execution (team: eng-evals).
- Model Owner: final approval on promotion PR and rollout gating.
- SRE/Observability: ensures telemetry hooks and alerting during prod-escrow.

Rollout checklist for model onboards that will use compressed few-shot sets:
- Add compressed primer reference in service eval-contracts.yaml.
- Include a quant-sensitivity job in CI that runs on PRs (INT8 + INT4).
- Instrument per-route token_savings_pct and fidelity_delta_by_slice to Redwood Console dashboards.

Troubleshooting and recovery patterns:
- If quant_sensitivity_score spikes after compaction: revert to previous primer and run targeted exemplar augmentation for the adversarial sub-slice.
- If safety sentinel failures increase: immediately pause roll-forward, revert serving primer, and open an incident with applied-ml and safety SMEs.
- If latency p95 increases during prod-escrow: evaluate whether decoder complexity from sketch encodings is the cause; prefer template-extraction fallback.

Auditability and reproducibility:
- All compression runs must produce a manifest: {seed_manifest_sha, compaction_steps, random_seed, compressed_set_sha}.
- Store manifests in eval-catalog S3 and link the compressed_set_sha from the model promotion PR.

Open questions and future work:
- Better automated exemplar-to-template extraction that preserves reasoning chains without hallucination.
- Compact sketch encodings that are runtime-decodable across different serving runtimes.
- Integration of exemplar-level explainability signals to prefer exemplars with clear token-level attribution for decisions.

References and related docs:
- See eval-harness throughput/fidelity playbook for aligning micro-canary sizes.
- See quantization-profiles checklist for mixed-precision rollout guidance.

Appendix: quick CLI snippets
- Fingerprint job (example):
  rci eval-fingerprint --input s3://eval-probes/seed/account-inquiry-v2.jsonl --embed-model embed-v3 --out s3://eval-catalog/fingerprints/account-inquiry-v2.jsonl
- Run micro-canary:
  rci canary-run --set compressed-v1 --size 256 --mode float --out s3://eval-artifacts/canary/account-inquiry/micro-2026-03-10.json

Contact and ownership:
- Document owner: Priya Natarajan (applied-ml)
- Reviewers: Marco Alvarez, Diane Chen
- For operational help: ping #eng-evals on Slack and tag the oncall evaluator engineer.

Change log:
- 2026-02-12: created initial draft.
- 2026-03-10: updated acceptance thresholds and added quant-sensitivity_score metric.

title: Multimodal function-call onboarding contract and priming budget specification
preview:
Summary:
This document defines the onboarding contract for services that expose multimodal function-call models to Redwood’s inference platform. It covers mandatory evaluation criteria, priming (few-shot) budget accounting, privacy constraints for example probes, and an operational roll-forward/rollback plan tailored to mixed-precision and quantized variants. The goal is to provide a deterministic acceptance gate for promotion from shadow to live, reduce regressions caused by priming-context changes, and standardize cost/quality trade-offs across tenants.

Scope:
- Applies to: any model suite that: accepts both text and images (or other non-text inputs) and supports the function-call API surface (structured tool outputs).
- Does NOT apply to: unimodal text-only models, embedding-only endpoints, or purely research validation artifacts.

Key definitions:
- Priming budget: the token + image-context budget (in token-equivalent units) assigned to each request for few-shot examples and system content. This is tracked per-route and per-tenant.
- Function-call fidelity: the probability-weighted correctness of structured outputs (fields, types, and side-effect intent) when executed by downstream toolchain. Measured as a composite F-score.
- Shadow cohort: an isolated traffic mirror used for onboarding where privacy-preserving probes + sampled live traffic are replayed against the new model.

Onboarding contract (must be satisfied for promotion):
1) Functional acceptance:
   - Unit-level function-call tests: 95% pass rate on schema validation for all critical tool signatures (strict schema enforcement).
   - Semantic alignment: cross-check produced arguments against a ground truth table of 500 seeded examples; minimum composite F-score >= 0.92 for production-tier customers, >= 0.88 for internal dev-tier.
2) Latency & resource constraints:
   - 95th percentile response < configured route SLO (default 450ms for interactive multimodal function calls).
   - Peak memory/KV-cache growth within 1.2x of baseline for warm paths.
3) Priming budget stability:
   - End-to-end latency and token cost measured under priming budgets (three representative budgets: small=64, medium=256, large=1024 token-equivalents) must not exceed baseline relative degradation thresholds (latency +20%, cost +25%).
4) Privacy & data handling:
   - No persistent retention of raw customer images or personal data in evaluator logs. Probes must be hashed and stored only when consented.
5) Quant & cache compatibility:
   - When quantized variants are used, per-slice embedding drift metric must be within the model-suite’s quant-fallback bounds (delta-embedding-cosine < 0.015).

Priming budget model (how we compute token-equivalents):
- Text tokens: 1 token = 1 unit.
- Images: compute token-equivalent by image complexity score (heuristic based on resolution and metadata). Default mapping: thumbnail (<=256px) = 16 units, small (<=512px) = 48 units, medium (<=1024px) = 128 units, large = 320 units. Teams may refine mapping during onboarding but must document the mapping in the model manifest.

Priming budget accounting table (example):
| Route | Default budget | Typical few-shot mix | Max per-request | Cost bucket |
|--|--:|--|--:|--:|
| chat-multimodal-interactive | 256 | 1 image (small) + 3 examples (each ~32 tokens) | 1024 | medium |
| batch-attachment-summarize | 512 | 4 images (thumbnail) + system prompt 64t | 2048 | large |

(Represent the above table in Confluence as a simple two-column table in the UI for maintainers.)

Privacy & probe design rules:
- Synthetic-first: prefer synthetic probes for rare PII cases. If using live-capture probes, apply on-capture redaction and store only hashed descriptors in evaluator logs.
- Consent recording: evaluator ingestion must include a reference ID tying each probe to consent metadata stored in the privacy ledger (see Related Links).
- Probe retention: default 90-day retention for annotated probes; retention extension requires Data Governance approval.

Acceptance tests and metrics (automated):
- Functional: schema-validation pass rate (see contract).
- Quality: composite F-score (field correctness, semantic match, argument completeness).
- Coherence: Response Stability Index (RSI) over 10K replayed anchors; delta <= 0.03 vs baseline.
- Token economy: per-1M request unit cost delta vs baseline <= +30% during initial canary window.

Canary and promotion procedure (numbered runbook):
1) Prepare shadow cohort: deploy candidate model to shadow environment with identical routing and priming budgets. Mirror 1–3% of production sampled requests (apply PII gating).
2) Run 72-hour probe suite: seed with 5k synthetic + 2k hashed live-capture probes, including edge multimodal permutations.
3) Automated evaluation: run the acceptance tests; generate an onboarding report (JSON manifest) with metrics and artifacts (diffs, counterexamples). Example report fields: model_id, route, composite_f_score, rsi_delta, priming_cost_delta, quant_compat_flag.
4) Technical review: onboarding owner and two reviewers must sign off. If any metric fails soft thresholds, iterate with remediation patches.
5) Phased canary: 0.5% live traffic -> 2% -> 10% over 24–72 hour windows, each step gated by automated metric checks.
6) Full promotion: only if canary windows pass for 48 hours with no severity >= P2 regressions.

Rollback quick paths (short checklist):
- Trigger conditions: schema validation failure in >0.5% of requests, composite F-score drop > 0.05, or latency spike > 2x baseline.
- Immediate action: open rollback via the rollout API, demote model, and activate previous stable variant. Notify SRE and Product.
- Postmortem: 72-hour incident review and regression triage meeting.

Monitoring and alerts (operational knobs):
- SLOs to instrument: schema-pass-rate (SLO 99.5%), functioncall-latency P95, composite-f-score rolling mean (7d), priming_cost_rate (tokens/sec), quant_fallback_rate.
- Alert thresholds: schema-pass-rate < 99.0% (P1), composite-f-score drop > 0.04 (P2), priming_cost_rate surge > 30% sustained 15m (P2).

Example onboarding manifest snippet (YAML):
```
model_id: multimodal-fn-v3
routes:
  - name: chat-multimodal-interactive
    default_budget: 256
    priming_profile:
      - type: example
        avg_tokens: 32
        count: 3
      - type: image
        size_hint: small
quant_compatibility:
  enabled: true
  embedding_delta_threshold: 0.015
privacy:
  probe_retention_days: 90
```

Failure modes and recommended mitigations:
- Priming explosion: if users send large numbers of examples per request, enforce route-level hard caps and return a 429 with guidance.
- Structured-arg hallucination: add a post-commit schema validator that rejects malformed arguments before hitting downstream tools and route to safe-fallback (no-op) with user-facing message.
- Quant-induced tokenization drift: maintain a shadow quantized variant and add automatic quant-fallback routing when drift metrics exceed thresholds.

Checklist (pre-promotion):
- [ ] Manifest filled and stored in model-registry.
- [ ] Shadow run completed and report produced.
- [ ] Privacy ledger entries for probes exist.
- [ ] Two reviewers signed off in PR.
- [ ] Canary policy configured and rollback variant set.

Owners and contacts:
- Onboarding owner: Marisol Ortega (applied-ml)
- Runtime owner: Ethan Park (serving-runtime)
- Privacy & compliance: Dina Sattar (security & compliance)
- SRE oncall: refer to the oncall rota link in Related Links

Related Links:
- Model registry manifest template: /confluence/applied-ml-and-evals/model-onboarding/model-manifest-templates
- Privacy ledger and consent API: /confluence/security-and-compliance/data-residency-and-retention/probe-privacy-ledger
- Evaluator SDK adapters: /confluence/applied-ml-and-evals/eval-harness/evaluator-sdk-adapter-patterns-for-private-and-dedicated-2026

Revision history:
- 2026-01-12: initial draft by Marisol Ortega.
- 2026-02-05: added priming budget token-equivalent mapping and example table (reviewer: Ethan Park).
- 2026-03-02: clarified privacy retention defaults and added quant compatibility clause (reviewer: Dina Sattar).

title: Operator Interactive Regression Debugger — Playbook
preview:
Summary:
This playbook describes the Operator Interactive Regression Debugger: a low-latency, human-in-the-loop protocol and toolkit for investigating production-quality regressions that show up in eval observability or customer signals. It complements automated bisect and probe tooling by enabling an operator or engineer to rapidly confirm causal factors, inspect intermediate artifacts (prompts, tokenization, KV cache traces), and run guarded micro-rollouts or targeted mitigations.

Scope:
- Intended for use by SREs, Applied ML engineers, and on-call evaluators responding to model quality regressions or severe fidelity shifts.
- Covers interactive investigation steps, CLI examples, automated helper scripts, telemetry checks, and safe rollback/mitigation actions.
- Not a replacement for automated bisect; use after initial triage if regression persists or is high-severity.

When to use this debugger:
- Alert example: response-stability-index (RSI) for 'billing-invoice-parsing' slice drops > 8% vs baseline within 6 hours.
- Customer-visible correctness regressions with high support volume or P0 product impact.
- Regression bisect returns ambiguous results or multiple suspect commits/quant profiles.

Key goals and guardrails:
- Fast, reproducible experiments (target: operator-to-insight in < 60 minutes).
- Non-disruptive by default: default mode is read-only inspection and simulation against shadow datasets.
- All writes (rollouts, toggles) require explicit two-step confirmation and audit logging (audit event emitted to /audit/logs/regression-debugger).

Ownership and roles:
- Primary operator: oncall SRE or evaluator (role: trigger and lead investigation).
- Supporting owners: model owner (applied-ml), runtime owner (serving-runtime), infra owner (eng-infra).
- Escalation path: if confidence > 70% of production breakage cause, escalate to Model Rollback channel and Product Impact lead.

Signals and preconditions (what to gather before interactive steps):
1) Alert payload: RSI delta, slice id, example probe ids, and time window.
2) Baseline artifacts: golden prompt set for slice, production model id and commit hash, quant-profile id, last successful probe timestamps.
3) Telemetry extracts: token-latency distribution, KV cache hit ratio, embedding drift score, and trace id for an affected request.

Required telemetry and fast checks (run these first):
- rwd-metrics query examples:
  - rwd-metrics query "RSI(slice=billing-invoice, window=6h)"
  - rwd-metrics query "kv_cache_hit_rate(model=v2, window=1h)"
- Quick thresholds to validate: 
  - RSI drop > 5%: high priority.
  - Median token latency increase > 50ms: possible runtime regression.
  - KV cache hit ratio change > 10%: suspect cache policy/serialization change.

Interactive investigation steps (operator workflow):
1) Isolate representative probes
   - Command: rwd-eval probe-select --slice billing-invoice --severity high --limit 20 --output probes.json
   - Inspect a sample of probes for immediate pattern changes (format, tokenization anomalies, hallucination markers).
2) Re-run probes against shadow model variants
   - Run: rwd-eval debug run --probes probes.json --model rwd-model:v2 --shadow-only --parallel 8 --output run-v2.json
   - Then run against previous model: rwd-eval debug run --probes probes.json --model rwd-model:v1 --shadow-only --output run-v1.json
   - Diff: jq -S '.responses[] | {id: .probe_id, text: .text}' run-v2.json > out-v2.ndjson; jq -S '.responses[] | {id: .probe_id, text: .text}' run-v1.json > out-v1.ndjson; diff out-v1.ndjson out-v2.ndjson
3) Inspect intermediate artifacts
   - Tokenization: rwd-inspect tokenizer --model rwd-model:v2 --input "<example prompt>"
   - KV cache trace (sample): rwd-trace kv --trace-id TRACE-12345 --limit 200
   - Kernel scheduling: rwd-trace kernels --trace-id TRACE-12345
4) Hypothesis generation
   - Use artifacts above to produce 1–3 hypotheses (e.g., quantized attention kernel introduced off-by-one, prompt truncation due to new tokenization).
5) Quick micro-experiments (non-disruptive)
   - Use local simulation flags: rwd-eval debug run --model rwd-model:v2 --probe probes.json --flags "--disable-quant-fallback --force-512-context" --shadow-only
   - If hypothesis relates to quantization, try a hybrid fallback: rwd-deploy canary --model rwd-model:v2 --quant-profile q-safe --target-pct 0.5 --shadow-beta
6) Decision matrix
   - If difference between v1 and v2 for critical probes > severity threshold (default 10% relative failure), recommend one of:
     a) Immediate rollback to v1 (if customer impact is P0 and mitigation urgent).
     b) Canary with reduced quant aggressively disabled and close monitoring.
     c) Patch model prompt wrapper (short-term) + medium-term model retraining.
7) Safe rollback checklist (if chosen)
   - Owner confirmation: model owner acknowledges rollback finger.
   - Runbooks: rwd-deploy rollback --model rwd-model:v1 --force --change-id RB-<ticket> 
   - Post-rollback monitoring: open-monitoring dashboard "rollback-watch" and alert on RSI for 30 minutes.
8) Postmortem and tagging
   - Create a short incident entry with probe ids, hypothesis, experiment artifacts, and final remediation. Tag model artifacts with label: "regression-2026-<shortid>".

Heuristics for blame (quick guide):
- If tokenization diffs align with changes in token-lexicon or new special tokens: blame upstream tokenizer/merge.
- If answers diverge only after 200 tokens and KV cache hit rate drops: blame KV cache eviction policy or cache serialization changes.
- If deterministic seeds produce nondeterministic outputs across runs: suspect scheduling/kernel regression in runtime.
- If differences appear only with quant profiles: suspect quantization thresholds or emergency mixed-precision fallback.

Automated helpers (scripts & services):
- rwd-eval fast-diff: produces slice-level delta report (path: /tools/rwd-eval/fast-diff).
- rwd-debugger-ui: dashboard that mounts probe population and allows side-by-side comparison (internal URL: https://console/internal/debugger).
- scripts/regression-hunt/auto-gather.sh — orchestrates telemetry gathers for an alert id.

SLOs and thresholds (operational):
| Metric | Alert threshold | Operator action | Escalation window |
|-------|-----------------|----------------|------------------|
| RSI (slice) | drop > 5% absolute or > 8% relative | Start interactive debugger | 30 min |
| Median token latency | +50ms | Inspect runtime traces | 60 min |
| KV cache hit rate | -10% | Inspect cache policy | 60 min |

Safe rollout recommendations:
- Prefer shadow-only experiments for hypothesis validation.
- Canary target size should not exceed 1% of production traffic unless safety validated.
- Any canary that changes quant profile must be labeled and limited to < 0.2% traffic until 12h of telemetry passes.

Audit and compliance:
- All debugger commands that mutate production state must emit audit_event with fields: operator, alert_id, action, before_model_id, after_model_id, ticket_id.
- Audit log location: s3://redwood-audit/regression-debugger/YYYY/MM/DD/<ticket>.json

Runbook snippets (examples):
- Start a focused debug session and gather probes:
  rwd-eval session start --alert RSI-4573 --owner priya.natarajan --mode interactive --out session-4573.tar.gz
- Re-run probes against previous artifact:
  rwd-eval debug run --probes session-4573/probes.json --model rwd-model:v1 --shadow-only --parallel 12 --out re-run-v1.json
- Canary deploy with quant fallback disabled:
  rwd-deploy canary --model rwd-model:v2 --quant-profile q-safe --target-pct 0.5 --reason "regression-debug" --ticket RG-4573

Post-incident tasks and knowledge capture:
1) Populate the regression index card (template path: /templates/regression-index-card).
2) Add annotated probe examples to the canonical prompt set for the slice.
3) If change was code/infra, open an ADR referencing the root cause and mitigation plan.

Appendix: Useful debugging commands and endpoints
- Telemetry queries: rwd-metrics query "metric(args)"
- Trace viewer: https://console/internal/traces?trace_id=TRACE-<id>
- Debug UI: https://console/internal/debugger
- Audit viewer: https://console/internal/audit?source=regression-debugger

Related pages
- /applied-ml-and-evals/regression-triage/probe-farm-orchestration-for-rare-regressions-2026
- /applied-ml-and-evals/eval-harness/shadow-to-prod-eval-continuity-guidelines-2026

Contact and escalation
- Oncall SRE: #oncall-sre (Slack)
- Model owners: team-applied-ml@redwood.com
- Emergency Pager: PagerDuty — Redwood ML Critical

This playbook is reviewed quarterly or after any P0 regression event.
